title: Fixture: Silver-tier high-criticality UC for tests
dataSources: index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4625 from Splunk_TA_windows on domain controllers.
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), 9.x on DC forwarders.
spl:
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4625 earliest=-15m
| stats count AS failures BY src_ip, user
| where failures > 10
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.action=failure BY Authentication.src, Authentication.user
| rename Authentication.src AS src_ip, Authentication.user AS user
| where count > 10
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Authentication datamodel on index=wineventlog for 90 days.
description: Surfaces source IPs and accounts with more than ten failed Windows logon attempts in fifteen minutes on domain controllers.
value: Burst failed logons often precede password-spray or brute-force activity against privileged accounts. Early detection lets identity teams block sources before a successful compromise.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for computers that keep getting login passwords wrong many times in a row. When that happens, we alert the security team to investigate before someone breaks in.
implementation: Deploy Splunk_TA_windows to DCs, enable Security log forwarding, schedule every 5 minutes, integrate with ES notable or email action.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk_TA_windows on all domain controllers; WinEventLog:Security inputs enabled.
• Search head role with wineventlog index access.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable `[WinEventLog://Security]` on each DC forwarder; verify EventCode 4625 appears in index=wineventlog within 2 minutes.

Step 2 — Create the search
Aggregate failures by src_ip and user; threshold 10 is a starting point — tune per environment.

Step 3 — Validate
Run test failed logons from a lab account; confirm row appears. Compare count to Windows Event Viewer Security log on the DC.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to SOC dashboard; throttle alerts per src_ip for 30 minutes to reduce noise during pen tests.
visualization: Table of src_ip, user, failures; map panel if geo lookup available.
knownFalsePositives: Vulnerability scanners and misconfigured service accounts can generate bursts; maintain allowlist lookup for known scanners.
exclusions: Does not cover successful logons or Kerberos pre-auth failures on non-DC members.
evidence: Saved search fixture_silver_high_logon.json exported daily to secops index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Lab account triggers 11 failed logons in 10 minutes; search returns src_ip and user.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Single typo logon per user stays below threshold; search empty.
